thought: The write guard uses Redis for deduplication. If Redis is unavailable at startup or mid-session, the write guard may silently disable deduplication (fail open), allowing duplicate memories to flood Qdrant. This is the opposite of a safe default — a storage system should fail closed (reject writes) or at minimum warn loudly.
